name: Pico Quant
description: Advanced 3HP CV quantizer with 8 preset scales, quarter-tone microtonal accuracy, adjustable tolerance and glide. Supports clocked or free-running quantization and user-uploadable scales via web interface.

controls.name: Tolerance
controls.description: Sets the CV range around each note at which the quantizer advances to the next step.
controls.detail: CCW: 50% tolerance (mid-point advance) · CW: 10% tolerance (close approach needed)

controls.name: Glide
controls.description: Sets glide (portamento) time between quantized notes.
controls.detail: 0–1 second · at fast rates acts as wet/dry control

controls.name: Scale Button
controls.description: Press to cycle through 8 scale slots. Hold 2s to enter scale upload mode (LED blinks red).
controls.detail: 8 scales: Diatonic, Double Harmonic Major (Arabian), Harmonic Minor, Blues, Diminish, Pentatonic, Whole-tone, Chromatic

inputs.name: CLK IN
inputs.description: Clock input for clocked quantization mode. Quantizer updates on each clock pulse.

inputs.name: CV IN
inputs.description: Unquantized CV input. Negative voltages are ignored.

outputs.name: CLK OUT
outputs.description: Trigger output on each quantized CV change. Free-running trigger if CLK IN unpatched.

behaviors.name: Free-running quantization
behaviors.description: Without a clock, quantizer continuously maps incoming CV to the nearest note in the selected scale.

behaviors.name: Clocked quantization
behaviors.description: Quantizer samples and outputs a new quantized value on each rising clock edge, enabling rhythmically stepped CV.

behaviors.name: Scale upload
behaviors.description: Module enters upload mode (LED blinks red). Connect a device's headphone output to CLK IN and use the web interface at ericasynths.lv/quant to transmit custom scales.

patchIdeas.name: Melodic S&H
patchIdeas.patch: Noise → CV IN, clock → CLK IN, CV OUT → VCO, CLK OUT fires envelope.
steps: Press the SCALE button repeatedly until the LED turns dark blue (Pentatonic) — hard to play a wrong note.
Patch a noise source or random/slow-chaos CV → CV IN (keep it roaming between 0 and +8V; negatives are ignored).
Patch a steady clock (e.g. 1/8 notes) → CLK IN — the quantizer now samples CV IN on each rising edge instead of free-running.
Patch CV OUT → VCO V/Oct.
Patch CLK OUT → envelope trigger so the VCO is articulated every time the pitch actually changes.
Set Tolerance around 12 o'clock and Glide fully CCW (0s) for crisp stepping.
Start the clock — you get a random pentatonic melody in sync with the clock, never hitting a wrong note.
patchIdeas.signalOut: CV OUT — quantized V/Oct snapped to the Pentatonic scale (0 to +8V), updating on each CLK IN edge. CLK OUT — trigger on every CV change.
patchIdeas.hear: A musical random-pentatonic sequence that always sounds 'right': notes land on scale degrees, the envelope re-plucks each new pitch, and nothing plays when the noise stays inside one note's tolerance band.
patchIdeas.diagram:
PICO QUANT:
* Scale: Pentatonic
- Noise (Out) >> Pico Quant (CV IN)
- Clock (Out) c> Pico Quant (CLK IN) // samples on clock
- Pico Quant (CV OUT) p> VCO (V/Oct)
- Pico Quant (CLK OUT) t> Envelope (Trig)

patchIdeas.name: Microtonal melody
patchIdeas.patch: Upload a quarter-tone scale via the web interface, then quantize a sequencer through Pico Quant.
steps: On a laptop or phone, open www.ericasynths.lv/quant and click SEMI/QUARTER to switch the keyboard into quarter-tone mode.
Either pick a preset from the SCALES dropdown or dot in your own quarter-tone notes on the keyboard.
On the module, press and hold the SCALE button for ~2 seconds — the LED starts blinking red (upload mode).
Patch the laptop/phone's headphone output → CLK IN, pick a slot from the UPLOAD dropdown (matching an LED color), and hit UPLOAD on the page. The LED blinks green once when the transfer succeeds.
Unpatch the headphones from CLK IN; short-press SCALE to cycle to the slot you just filled.
Patch a sequencer CV → CV IN and CV OUT → VCO V/Oct.
Leave CLK IN unpatched so the module free-runs; nudge Glide up slightly so microtonal steps aren't jarring.
patchIdeas.signalOut: CV OUT — quantized V/Oct using ¼-semitone intervals (0 to +8V). CLK OUT — trigger each time the quarter-tone step changes.
patchIdeas.hear: Non-Western, 'between-the-frets' melodies: the VCO lands on pitches that don't exist on a 12-TET keyboard, giving maqam-like or xenharmonic colors while still following the sequencer's contour.
patchIdeas.diagram:
PICO QUANT:
* Scale: Custom quarter-tone (uploaded)
- Sequencer (CV) >> Pico Quant (CV IN)
- Pico Quant (CV OUT) p> VCO (V/Oct) // microtonal pitches
- Pico Quant (CLK OUT) t> Envelope (Trig)

patchIdeas.name: Tolerance experiment
patchIdeas.patch: LFO → CV IN, free-running; sweep Tolerance to change how the smooth LFO breaks into steps.
steps: Press SCALE until you land on Diatonic or Chromatic (lots of notes = more steps to hear).
Patch a slow triangle LFO (~0.2 Hz, 0 to +5V) → CV IN. Leave CLK IN unpatched so the quantizer runs freely.
Patch CV OUT → VCO V/Oct and CLK OUT → envelope trigger.
Start with Tolerance fully CCW (50% — wide bands): the LFO's slope is split evenly and you hear a smooth staircase climb/descend.
Turn Tolerance fully CW (10% — narrow bands): notes only advance when CV IN gets very close to the next scale voltage, so the output lingers longer on each step and 'jumps' late.
Turn Glide from 0 up toward max: at short glide times it acts wet/dry; at long times you get portamento between notes.
Compare Chromatic vs Pentatonic with the same LFO to hear tolerance shape more/fewer steps.
patchIdeas.signalOut: CV OUT — quantized staircase from the LFO (0 to +8V) whose step timing depends on Tolerance. CLK OUT — trigger every time the staircase advances.
patchIdeas.hear: The same LFO becomes either an even rising scale (CCW, wide tolerance) or a pitched 'hiccup' that hangs on notes and jumps abruptly (CW, tight tolerance). Glide turns the staircase into a portamento slide.
patchIdeas.diagram:
PICO QUANT:
* Tolerance: sweep CCW↔CW
* Glide: low
- LFO (Out) >> Pico Quant (CV IN)
- Pico Quant (CV OUT) p> VCO (V/Oct) // stepped melody from smooth LFO
- Pico Quant (CLK OUT) t> Envelope (Trig)
